pi_items.source: Task instructions and verifier.py logic explicitly target this output path.
Task instructions and verifier.py logic explicitly target this output path.
Execution of the Linux RasUnsteady engine on the USACE Muncie dataset confirms that results are generated into this HDF5 file path.
Python block in setup_task.sh and JSON loading in verifier.py both explicitly use this path.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the real-world Muncie, Indiana HEC-RAS model from the US Army Corps of Engineers (USACE). An execution of the HEC-RAS Linux RasUnsteady engine confirmed the expected HDF5 output file path and produced ground truth flow data. The three target cross-sections are 15696.24 (upstream, peak 21000 cfs), 9334.877 (midpoint, peak ~17937 cfs), and 237.6455 (downstream, peak ~20998 cfs). Using a 5-minute timestep (0.0833 hrs), all three cross-sections are mathematically classified as slow-rising (R_ratio > 1.3). The expected target paths for the output CSV and summary files match the task instructions perfectly.
